data: The text is placed on two lines over the entire length of the belt; the first line is 49 cm long, the second 57 cm.
second half of the 4th century BCE
Found outside of the city wall along the northeast side of the hill of Palikè by the local landowner Sig. A. Tranquillità (Gentili 1962).
I.Sicily with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana

An outstanding collection of archaeological material. The collection ranges from Siracusa and the surrounding territory (Megara Hyblaia, Akrai, Kasmenai etc) to encompass all of eastern Sicily, reaching as far as Gela and Agrigento. The ground floor covers prehistory through to the end of the Classical period. Upstairs includes Hellenistic and Roman Syracuse and a display of material from the catacombs. A gallery displaying material from Hellenistic eastern Sicily is in preparation.
Cinturone Eneo con Dedica da Palikè
G.V. Gentili, «Cinturone Eneo con Dedica da Palikè», Mitteilungen des Deutschen Archaologischen Instituts, Roemische Abteilung 69–70 (1963 1962): 14–20
The Sanctuary of the Divine Palikoi (Rocchicella di Mineo, Sicily): Fieldwork from 1995 to 2001
Laura Maniscalco and B.E. McConnell, ‘The Sanctuary of the Divine Palikoi (Rocchicella Di Mineo, Sicily): Fieldwork from 1995 to 2001’, American Journal of Archaeology 107, no. 2 (2003): 145–80
I Figli di Marte: Mobilità, Mercenari e Mercenariato Italici in Magna Grecia e Sicilia
G. Tagliamonte, I Figli di Marte: Mobilità, Mercenari e Mercenariato Italici in Magna Grecia e Sicilia (Roma, 1994)

Apparatus criticus

English translation

Valentina Mignosa

Phaikon dedicated (this belt taken) from the (men) of Kentoripe to all the gods.

Italian translation

Valentina Mignosa

Phaikon dedicò (questa cintura tratta) dagli abitanti di Centuripe a tutti gli dei.
